quiz_questions.question_text: What is torque?

options.text: A twisting force that causes rotation around a pivot point
A force that pushes an object in a straight line forward
The speed at which an object moves along a straight path
The weight of an object measured in kilograms of mass

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque is a twisting force that causes or tends to cause rotation around a pivot point (fulcrum). It depends on both the force applied and the distance from the pivot.

quiz_questions.question_text: What two factors determine the amount of torque?

options.text: Mass and volume of the rotating object
Force applied and distance from the pivot
Temperature and pressure of the environment
Color and shape of the rotating object here

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque = force x distance from pivot. A larger force or a greater distance from the pivot produces more torque. This is why using a longer wrench makes turning a bolt easier.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the formula for torque?

options.text: Torque = mass x acceleration
Torque = force x velocity
Torque = force x lever arm distance
Torque = mass x distance traveled

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque equals the force multiplied by the lever arm (perpendicular distance from the force to the pivot point). Torque is measured in newton-meters (N-m).

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the pivot point also called?

options.text: The fulcrum of the rotating system
The weight of the rotating object
The velocity of the rotation spin
The mass of the rotating body here

quiz_questions.explanation: The pivot point is also called the fulcrum. It is the fixed point around which an object rotates. A seesaw rotates around its fulcrum in the middle.

quiz_questions.question_text: Why does a longer wrench make it easier to turn a bolt?

options.text: It provides a greater distance from the pivot, increasing torque
It makes the bolt lighter so it turns more easily and freely
It decreases friction between the wrench and the bolt surfaces
It applies less force to the bolt making it rotate more easily

quiz_questions.explanation: A longer wrench increases the lever arm distance. Since torque = force x distance, a greater distance means more torque with the same force. This makes turning the bolt easier.

quiz_questions.question_text: What unit is torque measured in?

options.text: Kilograms per meter
Meters per second squared
Newton-meters (N-m)
Watts per second value

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque is measured in newton-meters (N-m), since it equals force (newtons) multiplied by distance (meters). This is different from joules, even though both are N-m, because torque involves rotation.

quiz_questions.question_text: If you push on a door near the hinge, is it easy or hard to open?

options.text: Easy, because you are close to the pivot point
Hard, because the lever arm distance is very small
Easy, because less force is needed close to the hinge
Hard, because the door gets heavier near the hinge

quiz_questions.explanation: Pushing near the hinge means a very small lever arm distance, which produces little torque. You would need a very large force to open the door. This is why door handles are placed far from the hinge.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is rotational motion?

options.text: Motion where an object spins around an axis or pivot point
Motion where an object moves in a straight line forward
Motion where an object moves up and down repeatedly
Motion where an object stays perfectly still at all times

quiz_questions.explanation: Rotational motion is when an object spins or rotates around a fixed axis or pivot point. Examples include a spinning top, a turning wheel, and Earth rotating on its axis.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a balanced seesaw, what is true about the torques on each side?

options.text: The torques are equal and opposite for balance
The heavier side always has less torque value
Only one side produces any torque on the seesaw
The torques are unrelated to the balance of it

quiz_questions.explanation: For a seesaw to balance, the clockwise torque must equal the counterclockwise torque. This means force x distance on one side equals force x distance on the other side.

quiz_questions.question_text: A force of 20 N is applied 0.5 m from a pivot. What is the torque?

options.text: 40 newton-meters of torque
10 newton-meters of torque
0.025 newton-meters of torque
20.5 newton-meters of torque

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque = force x distance = 20 N x 0.5 m = 10 N-m. The torque is 10 newton-meters about the pivot point.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is torque?

options.text: The measure of a force's ability to cause rotation
The speed at which an object moves along a path
The total mass of a rotating object on an axis
The energy stored in a spinning wheel or flywheel

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque is the measure of a force's ability to cause rotation around a pivot. It depends on the force magnitude and the perpendicular distance from the pivot (lever arm).

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'lever arm' mean?

options.text: The perpendicular distance from the pivot to the force
The total length of the rotating object or beam here
The force applied to make an object rotate around
The mass of the arm of a person pushing the lever

quiz_questions.explanation: The lever arm is the perpendicular distance from the line of action of the force to the pivot point. A longer lever arm produces more torque for the same force.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'fulcrum' mean?

options.text: The fixed pivot point around which rotation occurs
The force applied to make an object rotate around
The speed of rotation measured in revolutions
The mass of the object that is being rotated

quiz_questions.explanation: The fulcrum is the fixed point around which a lever or object rotates. On a seesaw, the fulcrum is the central support. On a door, the hinges act as the fulcrum.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'clockwise' mean in the context of torque?

options.text: Rotation in the same direction as clock hands move
Rotation opposite to the direction clock hands move
Rotation that does not involve any torque at all
A type of force that prevents rotation from occurring

quiz_questions.explanation: Clockwise rotation is in the same direction clock hands move (from 12 to 3 to 6 to 9). In torque problems, clockwise and counterclockwise torques are considered in opposite directions.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'counterclockwise' mean?

options.text: Rotation in the direction clock hands move forward
A force applied directly at the pivot point itself
Rotation opposite to the direction clock hands move
No rotation occurring in any direction at all here

quiz_questions.explanation: Counterclockwise rotation is opposite to the direction clock hands move (from 12 to 9 to 6 to 3). When torques in both directions are equal, the object is in rotational equilibrium.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'rotational equilibrium' mean?

options.text: When clockwise and counterclockwise torques are equal
When an object spins faster and faster over time here
When no forces act on the object at all in any way
When the object moves in a straight line with no spin

quiz_questions.explanation: Rotational equilibrium occurs when the total clockwise torque equals the total counterclockwise torque. The net torque is zero, so the object does not rotate or rotates at constant angular velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is meant by 'axis of rotation'?

options.text: The imaginary line around which an object rotates
The force that causes the object to start rotating
The speed at which the object completes one turn
The weight of the object that is rotating around

quiz_questions.explanation: The axis of rotation is the imaginary line around which an object spins. For a wheel, it is the axle. For Earth, it is the line from North Pole to South Pole.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'moment of force' mean?

options.text: Another term for torque in physics calculations
The instant when a force first begins to act
The momentum of an object moving in a circle
The time it takes for one complete rotation cycle

quiz_questions.explanation: Moment of force is another term for torque. It refers to the turning effect of a force about a pivot point. Moment = force x perpendicular distance from the pivot.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'angular velocity' mean?

options.text: The rate at which an object rotates, measured in turns per time
The speed at which an object moves in a straight line forward
The distance from the center of rotation to the outer edge
The mass of an object that is spinning around an axis

quiz_questions.explanation: Angular velocity is the rate of rotation, describing how fast an object spins. It can be measured in revolutions per second, degrees per second, or radians per second.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'perpendicular' mean when applying force for torque?

options.text: At a 90-degree angle to the lever arm for maximum torque
Parallel to the lever arm for maximum possible torque
Directly at the pivot point for maximum torque value
In the same direction as the rotation of the object

quiz_questions.explanation: Perpendicular means at a 90-degree angle. Torque is maximized when the force is applied perpendicular to the lever arm. Pushing at an angle less than 90 degrees reduces the effective torque.

quiz_questions.question_text: A child weighing 300 N sits 2 m from the fulcrum of a seesaw. What torque do they produce?

options.text: 150 newton-meters of torque
302 newton-meters of torque
600 newton-meters of torque
100 newton-meters of torque

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque = force x distance = 300 N x 2 m = 600 N-m. The child produces 600 newton-meters of torque about the fulcrum.

quiz_questions.question_text: On the same seesaw, where must a 600 N adult sit to balance the 300 N child at 2 m?

options.text: 1 meter from the fulcrum on the opposite side
2 meters from the fulcrum on the opposite side
4 meters from the fulcrum on the opposite side
3 meters from the fulcrum on the opposite side

quiz_questions.explanation: For balance: child's torque = adult's torque. 300 N x 2 m = 600 N x d. 600 = 600d. d = 1 m. The adult must sit 1 meter from the fulcrum to balance.

quiz_questions.question_text: A door handle is 0.8 m from the hinge. You push with 15 N. What torque do you apply?

options.text: 12 newton-meters of torque
0.053 newton-meters of torque
15.8 newton-meters of torque
18.75 newton-meters of torque

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque = force x distance = 15 N x 0.8 m = 12 N-m. You apply 12 newton-meters of torque to open the door.

quiz_questions.question_text: A mechanic uses a 0.3 m wrench to apply 60 N-m of torque to a bolt. What force did they apply?

options.text: 18 newtons of force on the wrench
200 newtons of force on the wrench
60.3 newtons of force on the wrench
20 newtons of force on the wrench

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque = force x distance, so force = torque / distance = 60 N-m / 0.3 m = 200 N. The mechanic applied 200 newtons of force.

quiz_questions.question_text: Why are doorknobs placed far from the hinges rather than near them?

options.text: To maximize the lever arm and make opening easier with less force
To minimize the torque required to keep the door closed shut
To reduce the weight of the door for easier installation
To prevent the door from swinging open by itself in wind

quiz_questions.explanation: Placing the handle far from the hinge maximizes the lever arm distance. Since torque = force x distance, a larger distance means less force is needed to produce the same torque to open the door.

quiz_questions.question_text: A bicycle pedal is pushed with 50 N at 0.2 m from the center of the crank. What torque rotates the crank?

options.text: 250 newton-meters of torque
10 newton-meters of torque
0.004 newton-meters of torque
50.2 newton-meters of torque

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque = force x distance = 50 N x 0.2 m = 10 N-m. The cyclist produces 10 newton-meters of torque to rotate the bicycle crank.

quiz_questions.question_text: A spinning figure skater pulls their arms in close to their body. What happens to their spin speed?

options.text: They spin slower because of the reduced arm weight
Their spin speed stays the same regardless of position
They spin faster because mass moves closer to the axis
They immediately stop spinning when arms are pulled in

quiz_questions.explanation: When a skater pulls their arms in, they move mass closer to the axis of rotation. Due to conservation of angular momentum, this causes the spin to speed up. Extending arms slows the spin down.

quiz_questions.question_text: Two children sit on opposite sides of a seesaw. Child A (200 N) sits at 3 m. Child B (300 N) sits at 2 m. Is the seesaw balanced?

options.text: Yes, both sides produce 600 N-m of torque
No, Child B's side is heavier so it tips down
No, Child A's side is heavier so it tips down
Yes, but only because both children weigh the same

quiz_questions.explanation: Child A torque = 200 N x 3 m = 600 N-m. Child B torque = 300 N x 2 m = 600 N-m. Both torques are equal, so the seesaw is balanced even though the children have different weights.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student cannot unscrew a tight bolt with a short wrench. What should they do?

options.text: Use a shorter wrench to apply more focused force
Push the wrench closer to the bolt for more leverage
Use a longer wrench to increase the lever arm distance
Apply less force to the wrench for better control

quiz_questions.explanation: A longer wrench provides a greater lever arm distance. Since torque = force x distance, the same force applied farther from the bolt produces more torque, making it easier to unscrew.

quiz_questions.question_text: A painter carries a bucket of paint at the end of a 1.5 m pole balanced on their shoulder. The bucket weighs 40 N. What torque does the bucket create about the shoulder?

options.text: 26.7 newton-meters of torque
41.5 newton-meters of torque
60 newton-meters of torque
0.0375 newton-meters of torque

quiz_questions.explanation: Torque = force x distance = 40 N x 1.5 m = 60 N-m. The bucket creates 60 newton-meters of torque about the painter's shoulder.
